# Cruxible Docs

> Cruxible is one shared truth for humans and AI agents: a typed state layer where every write is governed before it is accepted and every answer carries a receipt. Open source, Apache 2.0. Kit catalog: https://cruxible.ai/kits · Skills: https://cruxible.ai/skills

- [Home](https://docs.cruxible.ai/): Cruxible is one shared truth for humans and AI agents: a typed state layer where every write is governed before it's accepted and every answer comes back with a receipt. These docs are built from the repository's docs/ d
## Getting started

- [Quickstart](https://docs.cruxible.ai/quickstart/): Get from install to a governed kit-backed state model in a few minutes.
- [Concepts](https://docs.cruxible.ai/concepts/): Cruxible is a deterministic state runtime with receipts. It gives agents and humans a shared, governed substrate for domain state that should survive beyond one prompt, one chat, or one run.
- [Modeling state](https://docs.cruxible.ai/modeling-state/): Cruxible holds durable, shared state that both people and AI agents work from. When you decide what to put in that state, every piece of information you add plays one of three roles. You don't label the role directly — i
- [For AI agents](https://docs.cruxible.ai/for-ai-agents/): This guide is the operating playbook for agents that use Cruxible. The agent supplies interpretation and planning. Cruxible supplies deterministic execution, governed state transitions, receipts, traces, review groups, a
## Kits

- [Kit walkthroughs](https://docs.cruxible.ai/kit-walkthroughs/): This page shows the two common authoring paths for 0.2: create a standalone kit from scratch, and customize an overlay kit on top of an existing reference state.
- [Kit authoring](https://docs.cruxible.ai/kit-authoring/): A Cruxible kit is a versioned bundle with a cruxible-kit.yaml manifest, an entry config, provider code, optional data, and a bundled cruxible.lock.yaml.
- [KEV guide](https://docs.cruxible.ai/kev-guide/): The KEV pair turns CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog into a governed triage brain over **your** assets. Two kits, two roles:
## Reference

- [CLI reference](https://docs.cruxible.ai/cli-reference/): This is the full searchable reference for the cruxible command line. Walkthroughs and agent recipes live elsewhere; this file is intentionally detailed so an agent can look up command names, flags, side effects, and fail
- [Config reference](https://docs.cruxible.ai/config-reference/): Cruxible configs are YAML files that define a decision domain: entity types, relationships, named queries, constraints, workflows, providers, artifacts, quality checks, feedback profiles, outcome profiles, and decision p
- [MCP tools](https://docs.cruxible.ai/mcp-tools/): This is the full searchable reference for Cruxible MCP tools. MCP is a curated agent connector, not full CLI parity. The HTTP API/client remain the broader remote product surface; CLI keeps shell-only utilities such as c
- [Common providers](https://docs.cruxible.ai/common-providers/): Cruxible has two reusable mechanisms that can look similar in configs but serve different purposes.
## Operations

- [Local state and backups](https://docs.cruxible.ai/local-state-and-backups/): SQLite is an acceptable starting point for OSS, local daemon, and single-droplet deployments. The important rule is that all state changes still go through Cruxible surfaces: workflows, canonical apply, proposal groups, 
- [Auth and agent roles](https://docs.cruxible.ai/runtime-auth-and-agent-roles/): Cruxible can run as a local library, but agent workflows that rely on review gates should run through an authenticated daemon. The daemon owns state and credentials. Agents use scoped runtime credentials to read, write, 
- [State resolution and maintenance](https://docs.cruxible.ai/state-resolution-and-maintenance/): This document is for adopters who have run the Quickstart and now need to trust Cruxible with real state. It answers two questions from the runtime's actual behavior: when agents and pipelines disagree, what wins — and w
- [Publishing states](https://docs.cruxible.ai/publishing-states/): A reference state is a published, versioned release of an instance's state that other instances subscribe to and track. Cruxible publishes the KEV reference this way (consume it via the KEV Guide); this page is the gener
- [Isolated deployment](https://docs.cruxible.ai/isolated-deployment/): This guide is the advanced path for users who want a real runtime boundary between the agent and the Cruxible graph.
- [Hosted runtime image](https://docs.cruxible.ai/hosted-runtime-image/): The hosted runtime image packages cruxible (daemon included) for private runtime containers. It starts the daemon (cruxible server start) as a non-root cruxible user and stores mutable server state under /var/lib/cruxibl
